metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` is a valid `?o=` option on the running Gerrit version (or replace it with the correct API — e.g., `GET /changes/{id}/related` — and update the AC with the confirmed endpoint name and field path.
findings.finding: The plan's rebase-selection rule cites 'GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION + the related-changes / SUBMITTED_TOGETHER set' to detect relation-chain membership, but SUBMITTED_TOGETHER does not appear anywhere in the codebase (src/, docs/, infra/). If this is a Gerrit change-detail option (like CURRENT_REVISION), its availability on the running Gerrit version is unverifiable; if the option name or the API path is wrong, the chain-detection logic — and the entire rebase-selection branch — will silently fail to detect multi-member chains.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule (second AC bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the related-changes / SUBMITTED_TOGETHER set' with a verified endpoint: `GET /changes/{id}/related` returns a `changes[]` array with `_change_number`, `status`, and `commit` fields; use that to enumerate chain members. Confirm against the live Gerrit 3.14 API docs and add a note to the AC citing the endpoint.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify 'on_behalf_of_uploader' is a real field in the Gerrit RebaseInput REST body for the running version, and document the exact JSON body shape in the AC; if the field name differs, correct it before implementation.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer' as a parameter of POST /changes/{id}/rebase and POST /rebase:chain, but the string 'on_behalf_of_uploader' appears nowhere in the codebase (src/, docs/, infra/). The Gerrit REST API has an 'on_behalf_of' parameter for review votes, not for rebase — if this field name is wrong or inapplicable to the rebase endpoint, the DCO Signed-off-by preservation guarantee silently fails, and every rebased commit will carry the bot's identity instead of the original author's.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule (second AC bullet): 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Disambiguate AC2's chain-detection mechanism: specify exactly which Gerrit endpoint is used (`GET /changes/{id}/related` vs. `?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` on a search query) and what response field determines chain membership.
findings.finding: AC2's chain-detection mechanism conflates two distinct Gerrit APIs. The plan parenthetically says 'detected via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the related-changes / `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set' without specifying whether chain detection uses the `?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` query option on the changes list endpoint or the separate `GET /changes/{id}/related` endpoint. `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` does not appear anywhere in the codebase; `related-changes` also does not appear. These are different APIs with different response shapes and chain-membership semantics.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (Rebase-selection rule)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC2's merge-commit-chain-tip rule: a `--no-ff` merge commit at a chain tip cannot be driven via `POST /rebase:chain` (which operates on ordinary-commit chains only); specify that such a change requires a client-side re-merge onto the new `main` tip (producing a `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` patchset per ADR-0025), and clarify which category (chain-rebase vs. re-merge) takes precedence when a change is both a chain member and a merge commit.
findings.finding: The AC2 rule 'A merge commit that is *also* a chain tip is treated as a chain → use POST /rebase:chain' describes a mechanically broken operation: Gerrit's rebase:chain API is designed for relation chains of ordinary (non-merge) commits and cannot correctly rebase a --no-ff merge commit as a chain head. Applying it to a merge-commit chain tip would strip the merge object's second parent, converting it to an ordinary commit and destroying the feature-branch topology that ADR-0025 and ADR-0040 depend on. The correct operation for a merge commit at a chain tip is a client-side re-merge producing a MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE patchset, not a server-side rebase:chain call.
findings.location: AC2 — Rebase-selection rule: 'A merge commit that is *also* a chain tip is treated as a chain'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to add a third case: if the change is a --no-ff merge commit (regardless of chain membership), the autolander performs a client-side re-merge onto the current main tip and pushes a new patchset for review, producing MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE. Reserve POST /rebase:chain for relation chains of ordinary (non-merge) commits only, and POST /changes/{id}/rebase for single ordinary changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the complete enumerated value vocabulary for the `phase` field in the `wipChain` schema (e.g., `selecting | rebasing | awaiting_verified | submitting`) so that all decision rules that branch on `phase` have a shared, authoritative set of legal states.
findings.finding: The `phase` field in the wipChain schema (AC5) is named but its value vocabulary is never defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (`wipChain` schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `phase` enum table to AC5, e.g.: `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting}`; annotate which AC drives each transition (AC2→rebasing, AC3→awaiting_verified, AC4→submitting or back to rebasing).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the `re-drive rebase` protocol with AC4 and AC5: specify whether a TOCTOU failure resets `wipChain.phase` to `rebasing` (retry same chain) or clears `wipChain` entirely (re-select from queue), and update `tested_sha` atomically on each rebase attempt.
findings.finding: AC4's TOCTOU guard mandates 're-drive rebase' on SHA mismatch or `main` advancement, but the decision protocol for what 're-drive' means — which `phase` to reset to, what to do with the existing `wipChain` record — is not co-located with the guard rule or the AC5 schema.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard) and AC5 (`wipChain` schema)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the wipChain schema AC an enumeration of valid `phase` values and their transitions (e.g., IDLE → REBASING → AWAITING_VERIFIED → SUBMITTING → IDLE) so the field is verifiable.
findings.finding: The wipChain schema AC includes a `phase` field but provides no enumerated values or transition rules for it, leaving a deliverable sub-element with no covering criterion.
findings.location: AC: '`wipChain` schema' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a SPIKE (against the live Gerrit sandbox or a test repo) that calls `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` on a ≥2-member chain bearing DCO `Signed-off-by` and `rebar-ticket` trailers, asserts trailers are intact on the resulting patchsets, and documents the exact response shape — before implementing the serial lander.
findings.finding: POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain with on_behalf_of_uploader is a load-bearing endpoint the plan depends on for chain rebase + DCO/author preservation, but it has zero prior use in the codebase, no spike or probe is planned, and neither the endpoint's availability nor its on_behalf_of_uploader trailer-propagation behavior across all chain members is empirically grounded for Gerrit 3.14.1.
findings.location: AC2 — 'if the change heads a relation chain with >1 member … use `POST /rebase:chain`; … `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Create `infra/spikes/rebase_chain_probe.py` that: (1) creates two stacked test changes with DCO trailers, (2) calls POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase:chain with on_behalf_of_uploader=true, (3) fetches the resulting patchset commit messages and asserts Signed-off-by and rebar-ticket are present. Record the response schema (single ChangeInfo vs list) to fix AC5's tested_sha update logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` is a valid Gerrit 3.14.1 changes-detail option or whether chain membership requires `GET /changes/{id}/related`, and update AC2's endpoint specification to name the exact verified endpoint + option string.
findings.finding: The plan names `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` as a query option (via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set) for chain detection, but this option string has zero prior use in the codebase and its availability as a `?o=` option in Gerrit 3.14.1 is unverified.
findings.location: AC2 — 'detected via `GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION` + the related-changes / `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` set'
findings.suggested_fix: In the spike, also call GET /changes/{id}/related and GET /changes/{id}?o=SUBMITTED_TOGETHER (if available) on a known chain, and record which correctly identifies all chain members. If SUBMITTED_TOGETHER is topic-scoped (not chain-scoped), replace it with GET /changes/{id}/related in AC2.

findings.finding: The plan introduces new live Gerrit API boundaries (POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit, Verified vote polling) but specifies no testing strategy at all — not even a thin acceptance test. With no stated live/end-to-end criterion, the story can be closed green with offline-mock-only tests that never exercise the actual Gerrit submit path, satisfying all ACs while leaving the live integration path completely untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) / Scope section — testing approach absent throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A smoke/integration test against a test Gerrit instance (or replay fixture capturing real Gerrit HTTP responses) executes the full select→rebase→await-Verified→submit loop and asserts the submitted SHA matches the rebased patchset SHA.' If live testing is deferred, name the child ticket explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing plan section covering at minimum: (a) unit tests for rebase-selection branching (chain vs. single vs. merge-chain), (b) unit/integration test for Verified-freshness check (exact SHA vs. carried vote), (c) a simulated TOCTOU scenario where main advances between rebase and submit to confirm re-drive triggers, and (d) FIFO ordering with two simultaneously queued changes.
findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new branching logic (rebase-selection rule, Verified-freshness check, TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering) but contains zero testing plan — no unit tests, integration tests, or deferred test child ticket — leaving all failure paths (SHA mismatch, stale vote, main advancing mid-flight, chain vs. single-change detection) completely unspecified for coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section

findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.finding: No end-to-end check is described for the happy-path core loop as it would run against a real (or realistic staging) Gerrit instance — the plan introduces a new automated code path (rebase → await CI → submit) but provides no criterion that exercises this path live, only behavioral assertions.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution'; Acceptance Criteria as a whole

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `wipChain` retirement rule: specify the exact event (successful submit confirmation, or explicit discard on failure) that resets `wipChain` to null and unblocks the selection loop.
findings.finding: wipChain retirement/reset path is unspecified: the plan never states what clears or nulls wipChain after a successful submit, nor after a recoverable failure that exits the current chain. Without a defined teardown, the record either leaks (blocking selection of the next chain) or the behavior is left to reader inference.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema bullet and Single `wipChain` bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'On receiving a successful submit response (HTTP 200 from `POST /submit`), `wipChain` is set to `null` and the selection loop immediately re-evaluates the `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` queue.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete detection protocol for the 'merge commit that is also a chain tip' case in AC2: specify the ordered evaluation of `SUBMITTED_TOGETHER` (chain check) vs. commit-parent-count (merge check) and state which condition takes precedence when both are true.
findings.finding: The rebase-selection rule in AC2 specifies that 'a merge commit that is also a chain tip is treated as a chain' but provides no detection protocol for identifying this combined case — the two constituent facts (merge commit detection, chain-tip detection) use different API calls that are not reconciled in a co-located rule.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 (Rebase-selection rule)
findings.suggested_fix: Specify: evaluate chain membership (SUBMITTED_TOGETHER set size > 1) first; if true, use `rebase:chain` regardless of merge-commit status. Only fall through to the merge-commit branch if the change is a lone non-chain change.

findings.finding: The plan specifies two rebase endpoint paths — `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` for 'ordinary + `--no-ff` merge changes' and `POST /rebase:chain` for 'relation chains' — but provides no decision rule for how the service determines which category a given change belongs to. A feature-branch merge commit that is ALSO the tip of a relation chain is both a `--no-ff` merge change and the head of a chain; the two categories are not mutually exclusive, and the Gerrit API field(s) to inspect (e.g. `has_related_changes`, change kind, subject) are unspecified.
findings.location: AC2: 'Rebase to tip: `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` for ordinary + `--no-ff` merge changes … `POST /rebase:chain` for relation chains'

findings.finding: The lifecycle of `wipChain` on termination paths (successful submit, rebase conflict, submit rejection, service crash mid-loop) is not defined; there is no stated retirement or recovery path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Single wipChain / single instance (serialized)' bullet; Scope — 'Failure paths … are separate stories'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'On service startup, wipChain is initialized to None (stateless restart); wipChain is cleared atomically after a confirmed submit response or when a failure path releases it (covered by failure-paths story, but startup assumption must be stated here).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC (e.g., a named pytest target, a scripted Gerrit API call sequence against a dev instance, or a structured assertion on observable state) so that every criterion has evidence of satisfaction.
findings.finding: No AC has a concrete proving command or verification methodology; an executor has no described way to produce evidence that any criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all seven bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: For each AC, append a `Verified by:` line. Examples: AC1 → `pytest tests/lander/test_selection.py::test_fifo_order -v`; AC3 → `pytest tests/lander/test_verified_wait.py::test_fresh_vote_required`; AC7 → `grep -rn 'set_review\|cast_vote' project/lander/ | wc -l  # expect 0`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Scope whether the TOCTOU 're-drive rebase' recovery path is in-scope for this story (happy-path loop) or deferred — if in-scope, remove it from 'failure paths are separate'; if deferred, replace the AC's 're-drive rebase' with 'abort / signal an error'.
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly defers 'failure paths' to a separate story, yet the TOCTOU guard AC mandates a specific recovery action — 're-drive rebase' — on SHA mismatch/main advancement. This creates a cross-section contradiction: an implementer cannot know whether re-drive-on-TOCTOU is in scope or deferred.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC: 'FFO TOCTOU guard' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add 're-drive on TOCTOU' explicitly to the Scope as a loop-continuation case (not a failure path), or (b) replace 're-drive rebase' in the TOCTOU AC with 'abort the current wipChain and surface an error (handled in failure-path story)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a timeout (e.g., `verified_timeout_minutes`) to the 'await Verified +1' phase; on expiry, abandon the wipChain, emit a log/metric, and re-queue the change for a future selection cycle.
findings.finding: The 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' phase has no timeout or maximum wait duration specified. The loop can block indefinitely if CI never votes on the rebased patchset (e.g., CI outage, queue stall, vote misconfiguration), stalling the single wipChain slot and halting all auto-submission indefinitely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'If no fresh Verified +1 arrives within N minutes of the post-rebase patchset being created, abandon `wipChain`, emit a `autolander.verified_timeout` metric, and release the wipChain slot for the next selection.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default polling interval and maximum wait duration for the 'await fresh Verified +1' phase (e.g., 'poll every 30 s, give up after 30 min and release wipChain') so the executor can act without investigation.
findings.finding: The polling strategy for 'awaiting a fresh Verified +1' — interval, backoff, and maximum wait duration — is unspecified, leaving a defaultable gap the executor must silently fill.
findings.location: AC3 — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1 … before submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Poll `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_LABELS` every 30 s; if no fresh `Verified +1` arrives within 30 min, release `wipChain` and re-queue (failure-path handling deferred to the failure-paths story).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the autolander service reuses `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.GerritClient` for its Gerrit REST calls (the cleanest path), or — if the scope boundary genuinely requires a separate client — document why and extract shared HTTP plumbing into a shared utility to avoid NIH duplication.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new 'project service (Python, project infra — NOT src/rebar core)' that must make authenticated Gerrit REST calls (query submittable changes, POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, read Verified votes, POST /submit), yet is silent on whether it reuses the existing production GerritClient (src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py). GerritClient already implements authenticated urllib plumbing, XSSI stripping, GerritError, and is consumed by 4 production call-sites (voter.py, app.py, reconcile.py, dedup.py). If the implementer treats the 'NOT src/rebar core' scope boundary as a prohibition on importing review_bot.gerrit_client, they will rebuild equivalent HTTP client code — a textbook NIH duplication.
findings.location: What section: 'A project service (Python, project infra — NOT `src/rebar` core)'; AC2 and AC3 imply Gerrit REST calls to /rebase:chain, /rebase, and vote-reading endpoints
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Scope section clarifying that the autolander may import and extend GerritClient (or a thin extracted base from it) rather than reinventing authenticated Gerrit HTTP. The needed new methods (query submittable changes, rebase:chain, submit) can be added to GerritClient alongside the existing review-bot methods, or extracted to a shared `gerrit_http.py` in `infra/` — the 'NOT src/rebar core' boundary refers to business logic placement, not to reusing the HTTP client.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what happens to wipChain on service startup/restart: either (a) scan Gerrit for changes in a known mid-flight state and recover/re-drive, or (b) explicitly accept orphaned rebases and document the manual recovery step, with a startup log entry indicating the decision.
findings.finding: The wipChain is held in-memory only. A service crash or restart loses the wipChain state with no recovery path. If the service crashes after issuing `POST /rebase:chain` but before `POST /submit`, the rebased patchset is orphaned and the in-flight chain is silently abandoned — no operator signal, no re-queue.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'wipChain schema' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the detection mechanism for the 'awaits fresh Verified +1' phase in AC3: state whether the service uses REST API polling (and at what interval/backoff) or Gerrit SSH stream-events (and how reconnect/missed-event recovery is handled), so the implementer has a single directed approach to build.
findings.finding: The 'awaits a fresh Verified +1' phase (AC3) does not specify the detection mechanism — polling the Gerrit REST API vs. subscribing to Gerrit's SSH stream-events feed. This is a structural control-flow decision that materially affects correctness (polling lag can delay the submit cycle by the full poll interval after CI votes; stream-events is near-real-time but requires persistent SSH reconnect logic) and testability (polling is easier to mock; streaming needs event-injection harness). An implementer has no direction on which mechanism to build.
findings.location: AC3 — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1 … before submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the minimum Gerrit capability set for `autolander-bot` (e.g., 'Submit' on the target project, 'Rebase' — nothing more) and confirm it is NOT placed in the review-bot's credential group or any group granting LLM-Review/Code-Review vote rights.
findings.finding: The plan does not declare the access posture of the `autolander-bot` Gerrit credential — specifically, what Gerrit capabilities and label permissions the account holds — leaving it ambiguous whether the account is narrowly scoped (rebase + submit only) or inadvertently granted broader permissions (e.g., by reusing the review-bot's credential or group).
findings.location: What section: 'project service (Python, project infra)'; AC line: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the autolander-bot Gerrit HTTP credential lifecycle: add a named SSM SecureString parameter (e.g., `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token`), extend `fetch-secrets.sh` with its mapping, and add the corresponding env-var to `user_data.sh` — matching the established pattern for `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `autolander-bot` Gerrit service account that will hold a Gerrit HTTP credential crossing the internet-reachable Gerrit boundary (port 443/29418, cidr_blocks=["0.0.0.0/0"]), but specifies no SSM parameter slot, no fetch-secrets.sh entry, and no user_data.sh mapping for that credential — the secret lifecycle is entirely undeclared.
findings.location: Dependencies / Scope sections: 'autolander-bot vote ACL'; AC implying POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit calls
findings.suggested_fix: Add `/rebar/prod/autolander-bot-token` to `ssm.tf`'s `rebar_secret_params` list; add a `get_param autolander-bot-token` → `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN` line in `fetch-secrets.sh`; reference `AUTOLANDER_BOT_TOKEN` (not `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`) in the autolander service config so credentials stay separated and minimally scoped.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via Gerrit 3.14.1 API docs or a sandbox probe) that the per-label vote timestamp (`ApprovalInfo.date` or equivalent) is accessible in the `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` query response under a named query option, and name that option explicitly in AC1.
findings.finding: The plan asserts FIFO ordering 'keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp' but does not verify that Gerrit's query/search API exposes per-label per-vote timestamps in a queryable or sortable form. Standard `DETAILED_LABELS` query options return vote value and account; whether a per-vote timestamp field is returned (and usable as an ORDER BY key) is unconfirmed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'retrieved via `o=DETAILED_LABELS` (which includes `ApprovalInfo.date`) on the change list query; if the field is absent on a given change, treat that change as lowest priority.' Confirm the field name against Gerrit 3.14.1 `ApprovalInfo` schema.

findings.finding: The plan scopes the new service to 'project infra — NOT src/rebar core', but leaves ambiguous whether it may import from rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client. The new service needs Gerrit REST primitives (HTTP basic-auth, XSSI-strip, GerritError, urllib transport) that are already fully implemented in src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (6 existing production call-sites). If 'NOT src/rebar core' is read as 'cannot import rebar.*', the implementer will rebuild these primitives from scratch — a clear NIH violation against a production module.
findings.location: What / Scope sections: 'A project service (Python, project infra — NOT `src/rebar` core)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Scope: 'The service imports and extends `rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client.GerritClient` with rebase/submit endpoints; it is a standalone process in `infra/` but is not prohibited from importing `rebar.*` libraries.' This resolves the ambiguity without changing the architectural intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact Gerrit submit endpoint path (`POST /a/changes/{id}/submit`), any required body fields, and add 'autolander-bot identity holds Submit permission in `project.config`' as an explicit dependency or AC precondition.
findings.finding: `POST /submit` (ancestor-atomic submit) is a new Gerrit endpoint for this codebase — the existing GerritClient has no submit method and no prior codebase code calls it — and the plan does not state the exact endpoint path, required body, or whether it requires a specific permission beyond `Submit` in `project.config`.
findings.location: AC4 — 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA. … re-verify `is:submittable` … if `main` advanced or the SHA changed, do NOT submit — re-drive rebase'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a minimal error-disposition AC: 'On any unhandled API error or exception, log the error with wipChain context, mark the phase as `error`, release the wipChain slot, and emit an `autolander.error` metric — defer complex retry logic to the failure-paths story.'
findings.finding: The AC specifies zero error handling for any Gerrit API call (rebase, submit, SUBMITTED_TOGETHER fetch, pre-submit re-verify). The plan explicitly defers 'failure paths' to a separate story, but the AC as written contains no baseline safety net — not even 'on API error, log and abandon wipChain'. An unhandled exception in any step can corrupt or stall the wipChain silently.
findings.location: Scope section ('Failure paths... are separate stories') and all Acceptance Criteria bullets

findings.finding: The FIFO 'oldest-submittable-first' ordering key is unspecified — an executor cannot implement deterministic queue ordering without knowing which field defines 'oldest' (change creation timestamp, submit-ready timestamp, change number, etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'Selects the front label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable change/chain (FIFO, oldest-submittable-first).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'FIFO = sorted by Gerrit `created` timestamp ascending (lowest change number as tiebreak).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact Gerrit REST response field used as the FIFO sort key for the Autosubmit vote timestamp (e.g., `labels.Autosubmit.all[].date` from a `DETAILED_LABELS`-option query).
findings.finding: The Gerrit API field used to derive the FIFO ordering key ('Autosubmit vote's timestamp') is not named, so an executor cannot implement or verify AC1 without investigation.
findings.location: AC1 — 'FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Sort key is `labels.Autosubmit.all[n].date` (ISO-8601) where `n` is the entry with `value: 1`, retrieved via `GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit%2B1+is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact API-observable mechanism for detecting a 'fresh' Verified +1 in AC3 (e.g., 'the vote's `date` timestamp is after the rebase API call's response timestamp', or 'patchset number on the vote > pre-rebase patchset number') and address the NO_CODE_CHANGE edge case where Gerrit may carry the vote even on a rebase.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that a 'fresh' Verified +1 can be distinguished from a 'copied/carried vote from a prior patchset' by checking it against 'the exact rebased patchset SHA', but does not identify the Gerrit API field or mechanism by which the service detects whether a Verified vote on the current patchset was freshly cast vs. carried over (Gerrit internally carries `NO_CODE_CHANGE` votes silently — the vote appears identical in the API whether carried or fresh).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'fresh Verified +1 — defined as a Verified +1 on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset)'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC3, add: 'freshness is verified by comparing the vote's `ApprovalInfo.date` to the timestamp returned by the rebase API call (votes predating the rebase are stale regardless of patchset). As an additional guard, if the post-rebase change kind is `NO_CODE_CHANGE`, require the vote date to postdate the rebase call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact `?o=` option(s) required to retrieve per-vote timestamps for the `Autosubmit` label from `GET /a/changes/` in Gerrit 3.14.1, and verify this in the spike or against the Gerrit REST API docs.
findings.finding: The FIFO ordering AC requires reading the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp from the changes query response, but the plan does not specify which `?o=` option makes per-vote timestamps available, and the Gerrit changes-list endpoint does not return label vote timestamps by default.
findings.location: AC1 — 'FIFO ordering keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concurrency primitive that enforces the single-wipChain invariant (e.g., 'all wipChain reads/writes occur on a single asyncio event loop with no `await` between the null-check and the assignment', or 'protected by a `threading.Lock`').
findings.finding: The serialization mechanism for wipChain mutation is unspecified: the plan states 'single instance (serialized)' as a constraint but does not name the concurrency primitive (asyncio single-threaded event loop, threading.Lock, process-level guard, etc.). Without this, an implementer might introduce a TOCTOU race between 'is wipChain null?' and 'set wipChain = new record' if the loop ever uses async I/O or threads.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Single `wipChain` / single instance bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the wipChain AC: 'All mutations to `wipChain` (set and clear) are performed synchronously within a single asyncio task; no `await` point exists between the null-check and the assignment, ensuring no concurrent coroutine can interleave.'

findings.finding: The existing `GerritClient` in `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` has no `rebase`, `rebase_chain`, or `submit` methods. AC2 and AC4 require `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`, `POST /rebase:chain`, and `POST /submit` calls. The plan does not enumerate `gerrit_client.py` as an edit target, nor does it state whether the new service will extend this client or create a separate one.
findings.location: AC2: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase … POST /rebase:chain'; AC4: 'ancestor-atomic submit … POST /submit'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before full implementation, run a targeted Gerrit API probe (spike or RED fixture) validating: (a) that vote timestamp is available in the label detail response for FIFO keying, (b) how to distinguish a fresh vs. carried Verified vote by patchset SHA/number, (c) `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` behavior with `rebase:chain` on a real test change, and (d) the consistency window of `is:submittable` relative to HEAD advancement.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the correctness of multiple Gerrit API integration points that cannot be established by reasoning alone, with no spike or RED fixture planned: (a) that `Autosubmit` vote timestamp is reliably available and sufficient for FIFO ordering via the Gerrit query/detail API; (b) that 'fresh' Verified +1 vs. copied/carried vote is distinguishable from the API response; (c) that `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` correctly propagates author/DCO trailers through `rebase:chain`; (d) that the TOCTOU guard (re-verify is:submittable on tested SHA immediately before submit) closes the race reliably under Gerrit's consistency model.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — FIFO ordering AC, fresh Verified AC, rebase-selection AC, TOCTOU guard AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `spikes/gerrit-autolander-api-probe.py` script (or test fixture) that, against a test Gerrit instance: queries label vote timestamps, compares patchset SHAs before/after a rebase to confirm fresh-vote detection, and measures the latency between a HEAD push and `is:submittable` update — run before story implementation begins.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying a kill switch (e.g., an env-var flag `AUTOLANDER_ENABLED`, a Gerrit project config toggle, or a sentinel file) that halts the selection-rebase-submit loop without requiring a code deploy, and document the rollback procedure.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new service that will auto-submit changes to main with no feature flag, kill switch, or rollback path specified. If the service misbehaves (e.g., submits the wrong SHA, submits a chain that should not land), there is no documented way to disable it quickly without a code deploy.
findings.location: Scope section and Acceptance Criteria overall — absence of rollout/rollback specification
findings.suggested_fix: An `AUTOLANDER_ENABLED=false` environment variable checked at the top of each loop iteration that causes the service to idle (log + sleep) rather than select/rebase/submit is sufficient for a Phase 1 kill switch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the service-restart behavior for in-flight wipChain: either specify a recovery query (e.g., scan for changes in `WIP`/rebased state owned by autolander-bot on startup) or explicitly document that restart drops the in-flight chain and Gerrit will re-queue it.
findings.finding: wipChain is in-memory with no restart-recovery semantics: if the service process restarts (crash, redeploy) while in the rebase→await-Verified→submit window, the in-memory wipChain is lost. The rebased patchset now waits for a Verified vote that nobody is polling for, and the selection loop may re-select and double-rebase the same chain.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `wipChain` schema bullet; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the Gerrit authentication mechanism for the autolander-bot service account (e.g., HTTP Basic/Digest via `GERRIT_USER`+`GERRIT_PASSWORD` env-vars, or an OAuth Bearer token) and note any required Gerrit capability grants (e.g., 'Forge Author' for `on_behalf_of_uploader`).
findings.finding: The Gerrit API authentication/credentials mechanism for the autolander-bot service account is not specified, leaving a key operational constraint undefined.
findings.location: What / Scope sections (no auth mention anywhere in the plan)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out/rollback plan for a mis-submit (e.g., 'on detected erroneous submit, open a revert CL automatically' or 'alert on-call and document a manual git-revert procedure') so the irreversible submit step has a defined recovery path.
findings.finding: The submit operation is explicitly irreversible (a merged commit cannot be un-merged via Gerrit), but the plan specifies only a pre-submit TOCTOU guard with no back-out or rollback plan for a mis-submit — T4 requires the remedy to be an explicit rollback/back-out plan, not only a preventive guard.
findings.location: AC: 'Ancestor-atomic submit / FFO TOCTOU guard' bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or CLAUDE.md / design-doc section) capturing the serial auto-lander's novel decisions: the single-wipChain serialization model, FIFO ordering keyed on Autosubmit vote timestamp, fresh-Verified patchset detection rule, and FFO TOCTOU guard — at minimum a lightweight ADR stub before implementation begins.
findings.finding: The plan introduces several novel architectural decisions — the serialized auto-lander service pattern, the wipChain in-memory contract, FIFO ordering keyed on Autosubmit vote timestamp, and the FFO TOCTOU guard — but plans no ADR or design-doc update to capture them. The plan references ADR-0041 (an existing decision) but does not create a new one for decisions introduced here.
findings.location: ## What, ## Acceptance Criteria (wipChain schema, FIFO ordering, TOCTOU guard ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNNN-serial-autolander-core-loop.md` covering: (1) why single in-flight wipChain (serialization guarantee), (2) FIFO key choice (vote timestamp vs. enqueue time), (3) fresh-Verified definition (patchset SHA match, not carry), (4) TOCTOU guard contract (re-verify is:submittable immediately before submit). Reference it from the story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC requiring structured logging and at minimum two metrics: `autolander.wipchain.phase` (current phase label) and `autolander.loop.error_total` (error counter), emitted so operators can alert on stuck or erroring loops.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the autolander loop. There are no metrics, structured logs, or alerts for key events (selection, rebase issued, verified received, submitted, abandoned, errors). Operators have no way to see whether the service is functioning or stuck.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets) — absence of observability requirements

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC or validation note with a concrete observable outcome for agent sessions (e.g., 'zero concurrent in-flight submissions,' 'no manual rebase required after Autosubmit vote,' or an end-to-end dogfooding test confirming serial submission behavior).
findings.finding: No validation mechanism is named for the stated value: the plan identifies 'parallel agent sessions hand-rolling rebase and racing' as the problem but no AC carries a concrete before/after comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding step that would confirm the problem is actually solved.
findings.location: Why section; Acceptance Criteria (all ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Scope or add a new AC: 'Validated by a dogfooding run: queue two Autosubmit-labeled changes simultaneously and confirm only one submits at a time with correct FIFO order, no races logged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the output directory/module path for the new auto-lander service (e.g., `infra/autolander/` or `src/autolander/`) so an executor can locate the artifact without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The plan names no output file path or Python module location for the new auto-lander service. The plan says 'project infra — NOT `src/rebar` core' but names no directory (e.g., `infra/autolander/`, `src/autolander/`) where the new service code, its `wipChain` record, or its entry-point live. An executor cannot determine where to create the artifact.
findings.location: What / Scope sections

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (and a sibling `Dockerfile.autolander`) as downstream edit targets owned by the deploy story, or explicitly defer and reference them so the scope boundary is auditable.
findings.finding: `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` is an unenumerated consumer. The plan creates a new long-running Python service but does not identify that `docker-compose.yml` (the existing compose stack) needs a new service stanza, a `Dockerfile`, volume declarations, and a `compose-up.sh` seed step for the new container.
findings.location: Scope section / What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` (specifically the `want=` dict and group-creation block) as an edit target within S1's scope, so the new autolander bot group is provisioned before `refs/meta/config` is pushed.
findings.finding: `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict (line 123) is an unenumerated consumer. S1 must add the `Autosubmit` label and `autolander-bot` ACLs to `infra/gerrit/project.config`, which will reference a new group (e.g., a dedicated bot-only `autolander` group or extended `Service Users`). The `want=` dict in `setup-project.sh` must be extended to include any new named group; Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push if a referenced group is absent from the `groups` file.
findings.location: Dependencies section
findings.suggested_fix: In S1's scope, add a block mirroring the `feature-branch-drivers` / `Contributors` pattern: create the `autolander-bots` group if absent, add `autolander-bot` as a member, and extend `want=` with `["autolander-bots"]=1`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the 'Why' or 'What' section explaining why the single-process in-memory serialized wipChain approach was chosen (e.g., simplicity, single-instance deployment model, no cross-process coordination needed at this scale, failure-path deferral is intentional), distinct from merely stating the goal.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section justifies the goal (stop racing, serialize) but provides no positive rationale for WHY the chosen approach — a single-process, in-memory serialized wipChain service — is the right mechanism for this project (vs. e.g., Gerrit-side coordination via topics/labels or a queue backed by durable state). The absence of a stated positive rationale for the approach itself is a G6 criterion gap that leaves future reviewers and maintainers without a documented basis for the design.
findings.location: Why / Scope sections

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document a brief prior-art survey of lightweight existing Gerrit commit-queue bots (e.g., gerrit-queue, Gerrit's own `automerge`/`submit-queue` plugins, or equivalent) and explicitly state why a custom Python service is preferred over adoption — even a one-paragraph rationale in the plan or a new ADR stub satisfies this.
findings.finding: The plan jumps from 'not Zuul' (ADR-0040's cost rejection) directly to 'build a custom Python service', without evaluating lightweight existing Gerrit-native commit-queue alternatives that implement this exact loop (e.g., the Lishogi/lichess gerrit-queue bot, the Android/Chromium submit-queue Gerrit plugin, or other single-repo Gerrit auto-lander scripts). ADR-0040's call for 'a lightweight Gerrit commit-queue bot before Zuul' reads as an invitation to adopt, not build.
findings.location: ## What; ## Why; implicit gap between ADR-0040 §Consequences and this plan
findings.suggested_fix: Add a §Alternatives-considered subsection (or note in a new ADR stub) covering at minimum: (1) Gerrit submit-queue plugin — rejected because X; (2) existing open-source Gerrit bot scripts — rejected because Y; (3) why a custom Python infra service (same lang/toolchain as project infra) is the right fit given project constraints.

findings.finding: The `wipChain` state slot (AC5) has no defined schema, no specified transitions (when it is set, when cleared, and what it stores), and no placement in the service — leaving its structure and lifecycle ambiguous for an implementer.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5; What section

findings.finding: The `Autosubmit` label referenced in AC1 (`label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable`) does not exist anywhere in the codebase — it is absent from infra/gerrit/project.config and from every other config file — making it an undefined-but-referenced schema entity that an executor cannot implement.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `[label "Autosubmit"]` stanza to infra/gerrit/project.config (e.g. `function = NoBlock`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Autosubmit requested`, bot-only ACL) and grant the auto-lander service account `label-Autosubmit = +1` on `refs/heads/*`, or explicitly cross-reference the predecessor story that provisions this label.

findings.finding: The plan asserts `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` as the queue-selection query (AC1), but no `Autosubmit` label exists in `infra/gerrit/project.config` or anywhere else in the codebase. The query targets a non-existent Gerrit label, so the selection mechanism described in AC1 would return zero results against the live Gerrit instance.
findings.location: AC1: 'Selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain (FIFO, oldest-submittable-first).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `[label "Autosubmit"]` block to `infra/gerrit/project.config` (value = 0 No score / +1 Land this change; copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE; function = NoBlock) and a corresponding `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Service Users` ACL grant in `[access "refs/heads/*"]`, or split label provisioning into an explicit prerequisite story.

findings.finding: The definition of 'fresh Verified +1' is unspecified — the plan does not state whether 'fresh' means (a) the vote was cast on the current (rebased) patchset, (b) the vote was cast after the service issued the rebase, or (c) the vote timestamp is after patchset creation. Without this, the await condition cannot be implemented unambiguously.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1 on the rebased patchset before submit'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3: 'fresh = Verified +1 vote with `patch_set.number` equal to the patchset number returned by the rebase API call, not carried from a prior patchset.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
